samples.concept_summary: A technique where a function calls itself on smaller inputs, requiring a base case to terminate.
samples.source_text: Recursion solves a problem by reducing it to smaller instances of the same problem. A recursive function must have a base case that stops the recursion.

samples.concept_summary: An efficient O(log n) search over a sorted array by repeatedly halving the interval.
samples.source_text: Binary search works on sorted data. It compares the target to the middle element and discards half the array each step.
